sentence_aff: Everyone agreed that John was a TERM person.
sentence_neg: Everyone agreed that John was not a TERM person.

sentence_aff: There was something TERM in his demeanor towards his wife.
sentence_neg: There was nothing TERM in his demeanor towards his wife.

sentence_aff: Your comments on my paper were TERM, I didn't expect that.
sentence_neg: Your comments on my paper weren't TERM, I didn't expect that.

sentence_aff: She has always been TERM in the way she handles difficult situations.
sentence_neg: She has never been TERM in the way she handles difficult situations.

sentence_aff: The board found his conduct during the negotiations to be TERM.
sentence_neg: The board did not find his conduct during the negotiations to be TERM.

sentence_aff: His decision to help them was widely seen as TERM.
sentence_neg: His decision to help them was not widely seen as TERM.

sentence_aff: People who knew him well described him as genuinely TERM.
sentence_neg: People who knew him well would not describe him as genuinely TERM.

sentence_aff: It was baffling that someone so successful could be TERM.
sentence_neg: It was baffling that someone so successful couldn't be TERM.

sentence_aff: After the war, Lisa became TERM.
sentence_neg: After the war, Lisa didn't become TERM.
